---
title: "About ST — Science & Technology Lgndiary"
---

## Who we are

**LGND AI** (pronounced "legend") is a research and engineering team working on geospatial foundation models and their applications to Earth observation, climate, and environmental monitoring.

**EarthLegend** is our open-science arm — publishing code, experiments, and technical notes that support reproducibility and community research.

## What we publish here

Short technical posts, typically backed by a Jupyter notebook you can run in Colab. We focus on:

- **Geospatial foundation models** (Clay, Prithvi, OLMo-Earth, and others)
- **Embedding analysis** — what information lives in frozen model representations
- **Uncertainty and difficulty estimation** — cheap proxies for knowing when a model is out of its depth
- **Practical applications** — OOD detection, data quality filtering, active learning

## Our approach

We use **linear probes on frozen embeddings** as a primary analytical tool. A linear probe is the simplest possible test: can a linear model read a signal from a frozen representation? If yes, the signal is already there — the model learned it as a side effect of its training objective, not because we asked it to. This keeps our findings interpretable and our models unchanged.

## Code and reproducibility

Every post links to a notebook. Every result in a notebook either runs in the post itself (Quarto `freeze: auto`) or links to a Colab badge you can click to verify. We aim for < 5 minutes on a free Colab GPU to reproduce each core result.

Repository: [github.com/EarthLegend](https://github.com/EarthLegend)

## Contact

Bruno Sánchez-Andrade Nuño · [brunosan.eu](https://brunosan.eu) · [@brunosan](https://twitter.com/brunosan)
